word_list.word: children and fools must not play with edged tools
word_list.pronunciation: tʃˈɪldɹən ænd fˈuːlz mˈʌst nˌɑːt plˈeɪ wɪð ˈɛdʒd tˈuːlz

senses.definition: used to caution against engaging in activities or using tools that could be dangerous, particularly for individuals who lack experience or knowledge

examples.x: The workshop instructor emphasized that children and fools must not play with edged tools, stressing the need for focus, discipline and maturity when using power tools.

word_list.word: above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it
word_list.pronunciation: əbˌʌv ˈɔːl ˈɛls ɡˈɑːɹd jʊɹ hˈɑːɹt fɔːɹ ˈɛvɹɪθˌɪŋ juː dˈuː flˈoʊz fɹʌm ɪt

senses.definition: used to emphasize that it is important to protect one's thoughts and emotions, as they have a powerful influence on one's actions and behavior

word_list.word: better go about than fall into the ditch
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ ɡˌoʊ ɐbˌaʊt ðɐn fˈɔːl ˌɪntʊ ðə dˈɪtʃ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that it is better to take a longer or more circuitous route if it reduces the risk of danger or failure

examples.x: The business consultant said during the meeting that in a business setting, better go about than fall into the ditch, and always be prepared and make informed decisions to avoid costly mistakes.

senses.definition: used to caution against speaking without proper consideration or caution, particularly in situations where the stakes are high or the consequences of one's words could be serious

examples.x: "During wartime, careless talk costs lives, as revealing sensitive information can put soldiers and civilians at risk," said the military commander during the briefing.

word_list.word: discretion is the better part of valor
word_list.pronunciation: dɪskɹˈɛʃən ɪz ðə bˈɛɾɚ pˈɑːɹt ʌv vˈælɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that sometimes it may be wiser to exercise caution and avoid a potentially risky or dangerous situation, even if it may seem less courageous or brave to do so

examples.x: When the group of friends decided to explore the dark and eerie forest, John thought that discretion is the better part of valor, and decided to stay behind.

word_list.word: a live dog is better than a dead lion
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ lˈaɪv dˈɑːɡ ɪz bˈɛɾɚ ðˌænə dˈɛd lˈaɪən

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is better to be humble and appreciate the gift of life, than to pursue fame or power at the cost of one's own well-being or life

word_list.word: a postern door makes a thief
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ pˈoʊstɚn dˈoːɹ mˌeɪks ɐ θˈiːf

senses.definition: used to caution against creating opportunities for wrongdoing or crime, as even an honest person may be tempted to engage in wrongdoing if an opportunity presents itself

word_list.word: an ounce of discretion is worth a pound of wit
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈaʊns ʌv dɪskɹˈɛʃən ɪz wˈɜːθ ɐ pˈaʊnd ʌv wˈɪt

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is often better to be cautious and use good judgment, than to rely solely on one's wit or cleverness
used to imply that by securing or organizing something well, it will be easier to find or retrieve when needed
used to warn against overconfidence, suggesting that even those who are skilled or experienced can still experience failure or harm

word_list.word: it is the pace that kills
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ðə pˈeɪs ðæt kˈɪlz

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is often the speed or intensity of one's actions that can lead to failure or harm

examples.x: It is the pace that kills in today's fast-paced work environment, where employees are expected to constantly produce more in less time.

word_list.word: the highest branch is not the safest roost
word_list.pronunciation: ðə hˈaɪəst bɹˈæntʃ ɪz nˌɑːt ðə sˈeɪfəst ɹˈuːst

senses.definition: used to caution against blindly pursuing the most prestigious or desirable option, without considering the potential risks or drawbacks

word_list.word: those who play at bowls, (must|should) look out for rubbers
word_list.pronunciation: ðoʊz hˌuː plˈeɪ æt bˈoʊlz mˈʌst ʃˌʊd lˈʊk ˈaʊt fɔːɹ ɹˈʌbɚz

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of being prepared because even if one is skilled or experienced in a particular activity, unexpected challenges or obstacles may still arise

word_list.word: though honey is sweet, do not lick it off a briar
word_list.pronunciation: ðˌoʊ hˈʌni ɪz swˈiːt duːnˌɑːt lˈɪk ɪt ˈɔf ɐ bɹˈaɪɚ

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of considering the potential harm or danger associated with something attractive or tempting

word_list.word: the only free cheese (is|lies) in the mousetrap
word_list.pronunciation: ðɪ ˈoʊnli fɹˈiː tʃˈiːz ɪz lˈaɪz ɪnðə mˈaʊsɪtɹˌæp

senses.definition: used to warn that there is often a hidden cost or risk associated with something that appears to be free or easy to obtain

word_list.word: words must be weighed, not counted
word_list.pronunciation: wˈɜːdz mˈʌst biː wˈeɪd nˌɑːt kˈaʊntᵻd

senses.definition: used to emphasize that it is more important to consider the meaning and impact of what is being said, rather than simply focusing on the number of words being used

word_list.word: [mention] rope in the house of a (man who has been hanged|hanged man)
word_list.pronunciation: mˈɛnʃən ɹˈoʊp ɪnðə hˈaʊs əvə mˈæn hˌuː hɐzbɪn hˈæŋd hˈæŋd mˈæn

senses.definition: used to advise individuals to avoid certain topics or words in sensitive situations, showing respect for others' feelings and experiences
used to advise people to avoid doing things that could have negative consequences on their current situation or environment, particularly in the workplace or personal relationships

examples.x: Sarah had learned the hard way that dating a coworker was a bad idea, and so she always kept in mind to not shit where you eat when considering romantic relationships.

word_list.word: if you cannot be good, be careful
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf juː kænˈɑːt biː ɡˈʊd biː kˈɛɹfəl

senses.definition: used to say that if someone cannot behave or act in an ethical manner, they should at least be cautious and take steps to minimize the negative consequences of their actions
